<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS>>

THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS ARE AS FOLLOWS:

THE MLE FOR MU_X IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [THE MLE FOR MU_Y IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [THE MLE FOR SIGMA_X squared IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [THE MLE FOR SIGMA_Y squared IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [THE MLE FOR SIGMA_XY IS THE SAMPLE COVARIANCE BETWEEN THE PRE-TREATMENT AND POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES>>

<<THE HYPOTHESIS TEST FOR THE EFFECT OF THE DRUG>>

TO TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT THE DRUG HAS A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE, WE USE THE TEST STATISTIC BASED ON THE MLEs [THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE [THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE AN EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE [THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE T STATISTIC CALCULATED USING THE MLEs [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE Critical VALUE AT ALPHA 0.05 [THE REJECTION REGION IS THE REGION TO THE RIGHT OF THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE SIGNIFICANCE LEVEL IS 0.05 [THE REJECTION REGION IS THE REGION TO THE RIGHT OF THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE TEST STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [THE REJECTION CRITERIA
